title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Nasi eksperci z KPMG omawiają, jak rozpoznać restrukturyzację z perspektywy cen transferowych i wycen, jakie obowiązki raportowe (TPR, MDR) mogą się pojawić oraz dlaczego terminowa dokumentacja jest istotna. Omówione są praktyczne przykłady — przeniesienie produkcji lub dystrybucji, transfer pracowników, przeniesienie kontraktów — oraz końcowa checklista działań dla firm.
bullets: Definicja restrukturyzacji w kontekście cen transferowych.
Jak rozpoznać istotną zmianę i analizować wpływ na EBIT.
Znaczenie i wymagania dokumentacji restrukturyzacji.
Obowiązki raportowe związane z TPR i MDR.
Rola wycen, najczęstsze błędy oraz praktyczne przykłady przeniesień i transferów.
takeaways: Monitoruj operacyjne zmiany (produkacja, dystrybucja, zespoły) pod kątem wpływu na ceny transferowe i potencjalne obowiązki raportowe.
Przygotuj dokumentację restrukturyzacyjną na czas, by zminimalizować ryzyko i stres w późniejszych latach.
Uwzględnij wyceny i ocenę wpływu na EBIT we wczesnej fazie planowania restrukturyzacji.
Sprawdź, czy transfer pracowników lub kontraktów zmienia profil działalności i generuje dodatkowe obowiązki podatkowe/raportowe.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Marcus Oliver, lead of Google delta (Google's professional services team), argues the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a strategy — it isn’t about computing costs. He recommends reimagining value chains and deploying multi-agent systems to drive real business outcomes.
bullets: Speaker: Marcus Oliver, lead of Google delta (professional services).
Key claim: rushing to code without strategy is the costliest AI mistake, not compute spending.
Advice: escape the “coding trap” by focusing on strategy and value-chain redesign.
Tactic highlighted: deploy multi-agent systems to achieve real business outcomes.
Emphasis on aligning AI work to business value before coding.
takeaways: Prioritize AI strategy and value-chain mapping before committing to large compute or build investments.
Evaluate investments in infrastructure vs. investments in design and orchestration of multi-agent systems.
Ensure projects are tied to measurable business outcomes to avoid wasted engineering effort.
Consider engaging professional services or strategy teams early to avoid the coding trap.

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this replay episode reintroduces the kickoff of PwC’s Reporting reset series and covers foundational reporting and presentation topics for year-end financial statements. It walks through GAAP and SEC presentation requirements, period selection, balance sheet and income statement presentation, accounting changes and error corrections, and subsequent events, and includes links to related PwC guidance and time-stamped segments.
bullets: Re-release of the Reporting reset series kickoff focused on presentation fundamentals.
Covers GAAP and SEC presentation requirements and how to determine reporting periods.
Details balance sheet and income statement presentation, required disclosures, and best practices.
Addresses accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and subsequent-event recognition/disclosure.
Features PwC National Office speakers and links to relevant Financial statement presentation guide chapters.
takeaways: Review balance sheet classifications and disclosures for major infrastructure and capital assets to ensure stakeholder clarity at year-end.
Confirm income statement structure and period selection reflect your operations and material items so performance is reported consistently.
Document and disclose accounting changes, estimates, and any error corrections promptly to maintain transparent financial reporting.
Monitor and disclose subsequent events that could affect recognition or the users’ understanding of reported financials.
Consider differences in presentation and disclosure requirements if reporting under public versus private company frameworks.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description… DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions. It provides visibility to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure. Key features include AI Lens for visibility and policy enforcement, AI Red Teaming to stress-test models against real-world attacks, and an AI Firewall to secure prompts and responses in real time.
bullets: Unified AI security platform for agents, applications, and user interactions
AI Lens: visibility and policy enforcement across AI interactions
AI Red Teaming: stress-tests applications and models against real-world attacks
AI Firewall: real-time guardrails for prompts and responses
Available on Google Cloud (visit https://www.deepkeep.ai/)
takeaways: Visibility and policy controls can reduce unmanaged 'shadow AI' running on your infrastructure
Regular red-teaming of models and apps helps identify exploitable weaknesses before attackers do
Real-time prompt/response filtering can limit data exposure and operational risk
A centralized AI security layer simplifies enforcement and oversight for cloud-deployed workloads

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest, showcasing u/JeffersonPutnam’s small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The description lists the host (GeForce Dwight) and the full winning system parts list via PCPartPicker.
bullets: Contest winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — built an SFF GeForce RTX 5090 rig.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
CPU: Ryzen 7 9800X3D with 32GB Teamgroup T-Create Expert DDR5-6000 RAM.
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Host: GeForce Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato); full parts list linked on PCPartPicker.
takeaways: The build pairs a flagship consumer GPU (RTX 5090) with an SFF chassis, highlighting that high-performance GPUs can be integrated into compact systems—evaluate enclosure compatibility before deployment.
A 1000W Corsair SF1000 SFX power supply is used with the RTX 5090, indicating power delivery and connector requirements should be confirmed for dense or space-constrained deployments.
The inclusion of an AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) alongside a high-end CPU and GPU underscores the need to assess cooling capacity and airflow in small-form-factor systems.
System component choices (motherboard, CPU, RAM, PSU, cooling) are linked in a PCPartPicker list, providing a reference for exact part compatibility and specifications when planning similar builds.
